summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard captures an electrifying East Coast ’82 show with a smoking Playing > Crazy Fingers opener, stellar Touch of Grey and fiery Jerry Garcia leads.
review: The recording is a clean soundboard source that delivers crisp instrument separation, though the drums are noted as slightly harsh. Musically the concert shines – the opening sequence of “Playing” into “Crazy Fingers” is repeatedly praised, and songs like “Touch of Grey,” “High Time,” “West L.A.” and “Big River” receive glowing remarks. Overall it’s a must‑listen performance with excellent band chemistry.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both the musical performance and the soundboard quality across many detailed reviews.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean mix with excellent clarity; a few reviewers note slightly trashy drum tone but overall an excellent soundboard capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert bursts open with high energy on the “Playing”/“Crazy Fingers” sequence and maintains a strong, flowing momentum through classic mid‑set numbers.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers highlight Jerry ripping leads and delivering perfect guitar tone.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is described as thunderous, even shaking sub‑woofers.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s vocal rap on “Good Lovin'” and his playful banter (sailor comment) are noted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s funky organ riffs receive specific praise.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums are mentioned as a bit trashy sounding, but overall the rhythm section holds up.
